refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs focusing on one aspect each. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain analysis, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis without implying any implementation or combining multiple requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain characteristics of the worksite independently from other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and policy overlays
Provide an interface for planning and execution modules to query terrain information
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with safety and operational policies may introduce coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on generating a terrain model keeps the solution modular; overlays and policy integration can be handled in separate DPs or IMs to maintain independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate raw terrain data for modeling
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies
Potential complexity in ensuring the model is compatible with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the DP further to separate terrain modeling from policy and safety overlays to enhance modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital terrain models from site data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and site information necessary for terrain modeling.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain model generation is a core processing function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module primarily handles mobility and control; terrain modeling would require significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; terrain modeling is not central and would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services is unrelated to terrain modeling; containment is very weak with high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP, as it naturally contains the processing logic and data handling required, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect model accuracy.
Interface changes in Telemetry or Network Link could impact module integration.
If terrain modeling algorithms evolve to require real-time vehicle data, cross-module dependencies may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze terrain through digital modeling, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as the DP is highly contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (1).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately scoped to implement the DP, encompassing processing and generation of digital terrain models. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, indicating poor fit for this DP. Interfaces with Telemetry (input), Tasking (input), and Network Link (output) are well defined, supporting clear module boundaries and minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the generated digital terrain model can be validated against the acceptance criteria requiring availability of the model to planning/execution functions and inclusion of key site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the DP implementation and interfaces, ensuring traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor dependencies on Telemetry data quality and timeliness to mitigate risks to model accuracy.
Establish interface version control and change management for Telemetry and Network Link to reduce integration risks.
Plan for potential future cross-module dependencies if terrain modeling requires real-time vehicle data, possibly by defining clear interface contracts or shared services.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, minimal diffusion risk, and clear verification feasibility. The DP fully satisfies the FR with a concrete method, and the IM allocation is clear and well-contained. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable, with recommended revisions focusing on monitoring dependencies and interface management rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: To implement recommended revisions such as monitoring telemetry data quality and managing interface version control to mitigate integration risks.

assumptions: Telemetry data quality and timeliness are critical for model accuracy.
Interfaces with Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link are stable and well-defined.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness affecting model accuracy.
Future cross-module dependencies if real-time vehicle data integration is required.
evidence_needed: Verification results validating the digital terrain model against acceptance criteria.
Interface version control and change management documentation.
